channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Romanian director Cristian Mungiu's 'Fjord' wins top prize at Cannes
item.description: Mungiu took home the prestigious Palme D'Or for his film Fjord, a culture-war drama set in Norway.
item.content:encoded:
Fjord — and Sebastian Stan, during the awards ceremony at the 79th Cannes international film festival, in southern France, on Saturday.'/>

Mungiu took home the prestigious Palme D'Or for his film Fjord, a culture-war drama set in Norway.

(Image credit: Andreea Alexandru)
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster

item.title: Trump says a deal with Iran and opening of Strait of Hormuz are 'largely negotiated'
item.description: President Trump announced the unfinalized deal on social media after talks with Israel and other allies. It aims to end the war and includes two-month negotiations on Iran's nuclear program.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump announced the unfinalized deal on social media after talks with Israel and other allies. It aims to end the war and includes two-month negotiations on Iran's nuclear program.

(Image credit: Iranian Presidency Office via AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: The squeeze on Cuba now includes compensation lawsuits
item.description: American University professor William LeoGrande explains how the Supreme Court's decision to allow lawsuits seeking compensation for assets seized in the Cuban revolution to move forward fits in context of current political crisis on the island.
item.content:encoded: American University professor William LeoGrande explains how the Supreme Court's decision to allow lawsuits seeking compensation for assets seized in the Cuban revolution to move forward fits in context of current political crisis on the island.
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson

item.title: Severe global food crisis could come within the year, says UN agency
item.description: Maximo Torero, Chief Economist at The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations, warns that the war in Iran is choking the global supply of fertilizer and a food crisis could follow within a year.
item.content:encoded: Maximo Torero, Chief Economist at The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations, warns that the war in Iran is choking the global supply of fertilizer and a food crisis could follow within a year.
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson

item.title: Video shows Israeli 'double-tap strike' killed medics and a toddler in Lebanon
item.description: In Lebanon, an Israeli 'double tap' killed three medics and four others including a two-year-old girl. A neighbor's video shows what happened.
item.content:encoded: In Lebanon, an Israeli 'double tap' killed three medics and four others including a two-year-old girl. A neighbor's video shows what happened.
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf

item.title: What will Trump do next with Iran?
item.description: Three months since the start of its conflict with Iran, the Trump administration is oscillating between strike threats and diplomatic overtures. Neither path has yielded a clear resolution.
item.content:encoded:
Three months since the start of its conflict with Iran, the Trump administration is oscillating between strike threats and diplomatic overtures. Neither path has yielded a clear resolution.

(Image credit: Rod Lamkey)
item.dc:creator: Willem Marx

item.title: U.S. passengers flying from Ebola-affected countries rerouted
item.description: The U.S. government is responding to the Ebola outbreak in with travel restrictions. American citizens and permanent residents departing affected countries must fly into one of three U.S. airports.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. government is responding to the Ebola outbreak in with travel restrictions. American citizens and permanent residents departing affected countries must fly into one of three U.S. airports.

(Image credit: Michal Ruprecht for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang

item.title: NATO allies confused by Trump's unexpected move to deploy 5,000 U.S. troops to Poland
item.description: Some NATO allies say they are confused by President Trump's different statements on U.S. troop deployments to Europe.
item.content:encoded: Some NATO allies say they are confused by President Trump's different statements on U.S. troop deployments to Europe.
item.dc:creator: Teri Schultz

item.title: Chile's MAGA-inspired border control
item.description: Chile digs desert trenches along its northern border as President José Antonio Kast pushes a hardline migration crackdown critics say may have little effect.
item.content:encoded:
Chile digs desert trenches along its northern border as President José Antonio Kast pushes a hardline migration crackdown critics say may have little effect.

(Image credit: John Bartlett)
item.dc:creator: John Bartlett

item.title: Deadliest coal mine explosion in China in years kills at least 82 people
item.description: A gas explosion at a coal mine in China's northern province of Shanxi killed at least 82 people. Official news agency Xinhua said the accident happened on Friday evening. Around 247 workers were on duty at the time.
item.content:encoded:
A gas explosion at a coal mine in China's northern province of Shanxi killed at least 82 people. Official news agency Xinhua said the accident happened on Friday evening. Around 247 workers were on duty at the time.

(Image credit: Cao Yang)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
